title: Connection refused is returned as Connection reset by peer
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

 We have an haproxy running as podman rootless, when all frontends are disabled, it is expected we respond with 'Connection refused', but the response is actually  'Connection reset by peer'

  This can also be replicated running a container, and mapping to a closed port. The behaviour is different if the container is ran as root, or as rootless.

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
[root@testserver ~]# podman run -d --rm -p 9797:9797 nginx:latest
be6147859ae0ba6fa27ea3ef4e40cf5be47a807e2a67664f6c54197779a72ec6
[root@testserver ~]# curl localhost:9797
curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 9797: Connection refused


[user@testserver ~]$ podman run -d --rm -p 9797:9797 nginx:latest
f284b82329ba625c7805dbfaf15826df2d6aad051df063053da56672fae5e5e7
[user@testserver ~]$  curl localhost:9797
curl: (56) Recv failure: Connection reset by peer
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Running as root: Connection refused
Running as non-root: Connection reset by peer


**Describe the results you expected:**

Running as root: Connection refused
Running as non-root: Connection refused

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Is this expected behaviour?

Is there anyway we can respond with a 'Connection refused', with minor changes and still running as rootless? 
Apologies if this is not the right place for the question. Would https://github.com/rootless-containers/slirp4netns  be better suited?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Built:        Thu Jul 29 15:02:43 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

but also tested in an AWS EC2 with version 3.2.3

comments.body:
Yes this is a slirp4netns problem.
@AkihiroSuda Can this be fixed in slirp4netns?

> Yes this is a slirp4netns problem. @AkihiroSuda Can this be fixed in slirp4netns?

The default port driver is RootlessKit, not slirp4netns.

And this behavior is expected with both RootlessKit and slirp4netns, because they always listen on the parent namespace regardless to whether the actual port is listened in the child namespace.

I'm not sure whether we can solve this issue.

We are starting the podman container via systemd, so moving the systemd definition from the user namespace to system fixed this problem. The one concern is if this is an "official" feature, or just an implementation detail that could change in any version.

Thank you.
I think it is a implementation detail. If this can be fixed it has to happen in slirp4netns and rootlesskit so I am going to close the issue since there is nothing podman can do.
